get.summary: Fetch devices from the firmware service
get.description: Proxies a request to the firmware service to retrieve a list of devices.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Number of results to return (min: 1, max: 10000, default: 100)

parameters.name: offset
parameters.description: Index of the first row to return (default: 0)

parameters.name: search
parameters.description: Case-insensitive partial match across name, model, platform, and hwmodel fields

parameters.name: order
parameters.description:
Comma-separated field names for sorting. Prefix with '-' for ascending order (e.g., 'name,-platform'). Allowed fields: name, model, type, platform, hwmodel

Filtering options:

parameters.name: type
parameters.description: Filter by exact type. Example: coreimg

parameters.name: model
parameters.description: Filter by exact model. Example: Example: iPhone8,1

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: Filter by exact name. Example: iPhone6s

parameters.name: platform
parameters.description: Filter by exact platform. Example: s5l8960x

parameters.name: offset
parameters.description: Index of the first row to return (default: 0)

parameters.name: search
parameters.description: Case-insensitive partial match across filename, model, type, version and buildid fields

parameters.name: order
parameters.description:
Comma-separated field names for sorting. Prefix with '-' for ascending order (e.g., 'name,-platform'). Allowed fields: filename, model, type, version, buildid

Filtering options:

parameters.name: filename
parameters.description: Filter by exact filename. Example: iPhone_4.7_P3_12.1.4_16D57_Restore.ipsw

parameters.name: model
parameters.description: Filter by exact model. Example: iPhone10,4

parameters.name: type
parameters.description: Filter by exact type. Example: ipsw

parameters.name: firmware_assets
parameters.description: Filter by exact asset URL/ID. Example: https://wkms-public.apple.com/fcs-keys/rl_td3o_0EtXT8t6HvVJoeWXnoBuzumHyUTD8-MUaF8= -OR- rl_td3o_0EtXT8t6HvVJoeWXnoBuzumHyUTD8-MUaF8=

post.summary: Installs a firmware for cluster-wide use
post.description:
Takes an uploaded firmware image and installs it for cluster-wide use.  The firmware is then removed from Images.

In order to install a firmware, you need to follow a 4-step process.
First, POST /v1/images/ with { name: string, type: 'fw' } using content-type application/json to acquire an imageId.

Second, upload the firmware file via a multipart/form-data POST to /v1/images/:imageId.

Third, POST /v1/images/install-firmware with { imageId: string; filename: string } using content-type application/json to acquire a taskId.
   The 'filename' must be an exact match for the filename in the firmware service that you're uploading this image for.

Fourth, you can poll the state of the installation via GET /v1/images/install-firmware/:taskId.

post.summary: Installs firmware assets for cluster-wide use
post.description:
Takes an uploaded firmware assets blob image and installs it for cluster-wide use.  The image is then removed from Images.

This endpoint is synchronous: it forwards the asset JSON to the firmware service and returns the firmware service's response message directly. There is no task ID and no polling endpoint.

Installing firmware assets is a 3-step process.
First, POST /v1/images/ with { name: string, type: 'fwasset' } using content-type application/json to acquire an imageId.
name should be something like 'fcs-keys.json'

Second, upload the firmware assets file via a multipart/form-data POST to /v1/images/:imageId.

Third, POST /v1/images/install-firmware-assets with { imageId: string; } using content-type application/json. The response body is `{ message: string }` describing what the firmware service did with the assets.

get.description: Returns the status of an install firmware request.

post.description: Create a new Image. Accepts multipart/form-data (with optional file upload) or application/json (metadata only).

post.description:
Rotate device to orientation.  Accepted orientations:
1. Portrait
2. Portrait vertically inverted (up-side-down)
3. Landscape with top of the device to the left
4. Landscape with top of the device to the right

Sends a touch or button event to the VM.

- Buttons (or keys) to be held during the input are specified as an array of strings, each string must be either a single ascii character or one of the following keywords:
  - VM Buttons: finger, homeButton, holdButton, volumeUp, volumeDown, ringerSwitch, backButton, overviewButton
  - Keyboard Buttons: again, alt, alterase, apostrophe, back, backslash, backspace, bassboost, bookmarks, bsp, calc, camera, cancel, caps, capslock, chat, close, closecd, comma, compose, computer, config, connect, copy, ctrl, cut, cyclewindows, dashboard, del, delete, deletefile, dot, down, edit, eject, ejectclose, email, end, enter, equal, esc, escape, exit, f1, f10, f11, f12, f13, f14, f15, f16, f17, f18, f19, f2, f20, f21, f22, f23, f24, f3, f4, f5, f6, f7, f8, f9, fastfwd, file, finance, find, forward, front, grave, hangeul, hanja, help, henkan, home, homepage, hp, hrgn, ins, insert, iso, k102, kp0, kp1, kp2, kp3, kp4, kp5, kp6, kp7, kp8, kp9, kpasterisk, kpcomma, kpdot, kpenter, kpequal, kpjpcomma, kpleftparen, kpminus, kpplus, kpplusminus, kprightparen, kpslash, ktkn, ktknhrgn, left, leftalt, leftbrace, leftctrl, leftmeta, leftshift, linefeed, macro, mail, menu, meta, minus, move, msdos, muhenkan, mute, new, next, numlock, open, pagedown, pageup, paste, pause, pausecd, pgdn, pgup, phone, play, playcd, playpause, power, previous, print, prog1, prog2, prog3, prog4, props, question, record, redo, refresh, return, rewind, right, rightalt, rightbrace, rightctrl, rightmeta, rightshift, ro, rotate, scale, screenlock, scrolldown, scrolllock, scrollup, search, semicolon, sendfile, setup, shift, shop, slash, sleep, sound, space, sport, stop, stopcd, suspend, sysrq, tab, undo, up, voldown, volup, wakeup, www, xfer, yen, zkhk

post.summary: Inject a message into an iOS VM
post.description:
Given a message and source phone number, place this message as an incoming SMS message on the iOS VM.
For advanced usage, a raw message of bytes may be sent. In this case, the parameter should provide hex encoded bytes
 (0x00 0x11 0x22 0x33 in the example below) which are sent verbatim.
 The user must ensure that the body is the correct format for the underlying device stack.

post.summary: Mount and attach a storage device
post.description: Initiates an asynchronous mount job for a registered storage device. If already mounted, returns status 200. If not, queues a background job and returns 202.

500.description: System failure while queuing the job

value.suggestion: Check if the background daemon is running and folder permissions are correct.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the storage media

post.description: Initiates an asynchronous unmount job for the physical storage device. If the device is already unmounted, returns success. If mounted, creates a background job to safely unmount.

value.suggestion: Check if the background daemon is running and folder permissions are correct.

200.description: Returns the HTML file of the report

limit.description: Number of entries to return, defaults to 10

page.description: Paginated results. Must be a positive integer. If not provided, defaults to the first page.

instance.description: Instance identifier used to filter instance resources

project.description: Instance identifier used to filter instance resources

actor.description: Actor identifier used to filter actor resources

search.description: Last filter applied and is a fuzzy match on results

to.description: Date to filter to, keyed off of createdAt

from.description: Date to filter from, keyed off of createdAt

identifier.description: Unique identifier for the activity entry

metadata.description: Related information that pertains to the activity

corelliumVersion.description: The Corellium version this activity occurred in

projectId.description: Unique identifier for project if applicable

instanceId.description: Unique identifier for instance if applicable

createdAt.description: Timestamp of when the activity was created

updatedAt.description: Timestamp of when the activity was last updated

totalItems.description: The total number of activity entries

itemsPerPage.description: The number of activity entries per page

search.description: Arbitrary search string against activity metadata

to.description: Include activities up to given ISO 8601 Date

from.description: Include activities from given ISO 8601 Date

used.description: Is Used. If true, this coupon has been used and cannot be used again

total_usage_amount.description: Total cost in cents for current period

total_used_units.description: Total used units for current period

malware.description: option from sign up form, interesting using for malware

research.description: option from sign up form, interesting using for research

pentest.description: option from sign up form, interesting using for pentesting

other.description: option from sign up form, interesting using for other area added here

address1.description: Address line 1 (e.g., street, PO Box, or company name).

address2.description: Address line 2 (e.g., apartment, suite, unit, or building).

city.description: City, district, suburb, town, or village.

metadata.description: Metadata passed in from the sign up flow

enterprise.description: If true, create an enterprise domain.

count.description: Number of items in current response

extensionDays.description: Days to add to the trial before it ends.

extensionHours.description: Hours of usage to add to the trial. This will be either device-hours or core-hours depending on the trial.

nodeTypes.description: Types of services running on this node [compute,controller,gpu]

limit.description: Number of entries to return, defaults to 100

offset.description: Number of rows to skip, defaults to 0

springboard.description: Denotes whether snapshot was a springboard snapshot

live.description: Denotes whether snapshot was a live snapshot

fresh.description: Denotes whether snapshot was created immediately after the device was restored

to.description: Date to filter to, keyed off of createdAt

from.description: Date to filter from, keyed off of createdAt

search.description: Last filter applied and searches on snapshot / associated instance name and snapshot / instance id

limit.description: Number of items to return, defaults to 100, minimum of 1 and maximum of 1000

count.description: Number of items in current response

usbMediaAccessEnabled.description: Designates whether read-only access to removable USB media is enabled

snapshotLimit.description: Designates the maximum number of snapshots that can be taken in a cluster

coordinatorConfig.description: Partial coordinator configuration object to update cluster coordinator configuration.  Only supports updating usbMediaAccessEnabled for now.

message.description: Instructions to try again or resolve manually

nodes.description: List of nodes that failed to receive updates.

name.description: Provider name for a given provider type

enabled.description: Denotes whether they're enabled or not

onSite.description: Denotes whether it's an on-site install

showDomainSettings.description: Denotes whether to show domain settings

invalidBuild.description: Denotes whether the build is invalid

invoicedPublishableKey.description: API publishable key to use for Invoiced

intercomId.description: Intercom app ID, also known as workspace ID

zapierFeedbackWebhook.description: Webhook URL to send feedback into Productboard automatically

zapierBugsWebhook.description: Webhook URL to send frontend errors to Jira automatically

billingBackend.description: Default backend billing api name for new subscriptions (e.g. "stripe")

maintenance.description: Denotes whether the server is undergoing maintenance

cloudDomain.description: Cloud domain name (usually corellium.com or staging.corellium.com)

appDomain.description: App domain name (usually app.corellium.com or app.staging.corellium.com)

enableFirmwareImageUpload.description: Denotes whether users can upload firmware images

snapshotLimit.description: Maximum number of snapshots to allow

maxKernelSize.description: The maximum size, in bytes, (default: 100 MB) that an uploaded kernel should be

maxRamdiskSize.description: The maximum size, in bytes, (default: 500 MB) that an uploaded ramdisk should be

domainAuthenticationProviders.description: If enabled, adds the default providers in their current configuration

reason.description: why does the user want more time?

deniedReason.description: explanation for why the request was denied

cloudEnabled.description: Indicates if snapshot permissions are enabled by the cloud admin

domainEnabled.description: Indicates if snapshot permissions are enabled by the domain admin

publicLink.description: Indicates if public link access is enabled

domainRestrictedLink.description: Indicates if domain-restricted link access is enabled

passwordPublicLink.description: Indicates if password-protected public link access is enabled

emailInvite.description: Indicates if email invite access is enabled

id.description: Provider ID for backward compatibility with frontend

provider.description: Provider Type for backward compatibility with frontend

name.description: Title Text for the Frontend's Provider Configuration Form

authorizationUrl.description: Coordinator endpoint for Authorizing with this provider

default.description: True: Configured in coordinator.json, False: Custom Domain Provider

createdAt.description: Created Date in ISO-8601 format e.g. 2022-05-06T02:39:23.000Z

updatedAt.description: Updated Date in ISO-8601 format e.g. 2022-05-06T02:39:23.000Z

available.description: Indicates whether the subdomain is available or not

message.description: The welcome message text (HTML allowed, will be sanitized)
The welcome message text (HTML allowed, will be sanitized)

identifier.description: Unique identifier for the welcome message (UUID)

enabled.description: If true, instances requiring this extension can be created or started

createdAt.description: Created Date in ISO-8601 format e.g. 2022-05-06T02:39:23.000Z

updatedAt.description: Updated Date in ISO-8601 format e.g. 2022-05-06T02:39:23.000Z

enabled.description: If true, instances requiring this extension can be created or started

imageId.description: The uuid of the image to load

orig_url.description: URL firmware is available at from vendor

instances.description: Instances using one of the firmwares, and thus the asset

fallbackOriginalUrl.description: Use original URL to fetch firmware

downloadIpswFilters.description: Filters that control which IPSW builds are downloaded

enabled.description: If true, instances can call required hooks

createdAt.description: Created Date in ISO-8601 format e.g. 2022-05-06T02:39:23.000Z

updatedAt.description: Updated Date in ISO-8601 format e.g. 2022-05-06T02:39:23.000Z

enabled.description: If true, instances can call required hooks

message.description: Message returned by the firmware service describing the outcome of the asset upload.

corelliumChecksum.description: Corellium checksum of the image (format: algorithm$blocksize$hash)

encapsulated.description: set to false if the uploaded file is not encapsulated inside an outer zipfile

fullPath.description: Full local path to image file. When specified, this file MUST exist!

corelliumChecksum.description: Corellium Checksum of the uploaded firmware

error.format: eg: There is not enough storage space to store the requested file

total.description: Total image pool capacity in bytes

available.description: Currently available image pool space in bytes

required.description: Required space for the upload in bytes (from Content-Length)

firmwarePath.description: The relative path within the firmware to the kernel file that has been bundled up with the selected firmware

InstanceBootOptionsAdditionalTag.description:
### Instance Boot Option
* kalloc: Enable kalloc/kfree trace access via GDB (Enterprise only)
* gpu: Enable cloud GPU acceleration (Extra costs incurred, cloud only)
* no-keyboard: Enable keyboard passthrough from web interface
* nodevmode: Disable developer mode on iOS16 and greater
* sep-cons-ext: Patch SEPOS to print debug messages to console
* iboot-jailbreak: Patch iBoot to disable signature checks
* llb-jailbreak: Patch LLB to disable signature checks
* rom-jailbreak: Patch BootROM to disable signature checks

randomSeed.description: Random seed to provide to boot if any

stage.description: Restore stage: download-images, prepare, boot, start, extracting, asr, flashing, done

readOnlyStatus.description: Overall progress of flipping read-only state

key.description: Key used to encrypt the Instance

flavor.description: The flavor identifier of this instance

project.description: The projectId of the project this instance belongs to

domainName.description: Domain name associated with this instance's project

stateChanged.description: Time the state of the instance last changed

startedAt.description: Time the instance was started, NULL if currently SHUTOFF

secondaryIp.description: Secondary Interface LAN IP Address (if supported)

fwpackage.description: URL that package used to create this instance is available at

uefiBootloader.description: Whether UEFI bootloader is in use

mast.description: Whether the device has a mast

sharedSnapshot.description: identifier of the snapshot that was shared.

sharedSnapshotPassword.description: optional password if the shared snapshot requires a password.

key.description: Key used to encrypt the Instance

origFwPackageUrl.description: URL that firmware package used to create this instance is available at

state.description: the desired state of the device

devicePort.description: The device port to use for proxying.

firstAvailable.description: If `true`, the first available port will be used if `devicePort` is not available.

expose.description: If `true`, the proxy will be exposed to the external interface.

routerPort.description: The router port to use for proxying.

readOnly.description: The desired state of readOnly. Possible values: 'enabled', 'disabled'.

error.description: The error encountered by the agent

originalError.description: The full error encountered by the agent

frontmost.description: bundleID of frontmost app or empty string if none are frontmost
bundleID of frontmost app or empty string if none are frontmost

sockcap.description: Start device with Process Network Monitoring enabled

bitCount.description: count of bits in members of this bank

banks.description: bits for each bank of this name as an array of arrays

position.description: array of per-finger [x,y] touch positions, up to 10 depending on model

buttons.description: Buttons to be held when this position is reached. Either a Button enum object or an ASCII character. No change if not defined.

normalized.description: true if you want to use normalized x,y coordinates from 0-10000 (eg 5000,5000 is always center of screen)

wait.description: Duration to wait before this input is executed.  Instant if not defined.

start.description: array of per-finger [x,y] start touch positions, up to 10 depending on model

end.description: array of per-finger [x,y] end touch positions, up to 10 depending on model

bezierPoints.description: array of per-finger intermediate touch positions, up to 10 depending on model.  Straight line if not defined.

startButtons.description: Buttons to be held during this curve.

endButtons.description: Buttons to be left held after this curve.  Same as startButtons if not defined.

normalized.description: true if you want to use normalized x,y coordinates from 0-10000 (eg 5000,5000 is always center of screen)

wait.description: Duration to wait before this input is executed.  Instant if not defined.

duration.description: Duration to execute this curve over.

keyDuration.description: How long to take to type each key.  150ms if not defined.

protocol.description: The protocol used by the interconnect
The protocol used by the interconnect

status.description: The current status of the interconnect

count.description: Number of interconnects in this response

interfaces.description: Array of interfaces with deviceId and interfaceId
Array of interfaces with deviceId and interfaceId (optional)

ids.description: id or array of ids to revoke

connection.description: UUIDv4 network connection identifier or null for no vpn connection

state.description: Current state of the network connection

inviteSentAt.description: The date when the invite was sent

sharedOn.description: UNIX Date of when the snapshot was first shared with member

members.description: The members who have access to the snapshot

task.description: The current task being performed on the snapshot

created.description: Indicates if the snapshot has been successfully created

sharedOn.description: UNIX Date of when the snapshot was first shared with member

sharedWithMember.description: The member who the snapshot was shared with. Only present in sharedWithUser

sharedWithUser.description: Snapshots shared with the logged-in user

sharedByUser.description: Snapshots shared by the logged-in user

instance.description: Instance that this snapshot is of

date.description: UNIX Date that the snapshot was created

sharing.description: The sharing information of the snapshot

members.description: The email addresses or user ids of the members.

count.description: Number of storage devices in this response

administrator.description: the flag that specifies whether user is Administrator or not

canEditUserAttributes.description: Flag to determine if user attributes are editable.

clusterAdministrator.description: the flag that specifies whether user is cluster administrator or not

administrator.description: Whether the user is a domain administrator. Only users with the Domain Administrator role can set this.

clusterAdministrator.description: Whether the user is a cluster administrator. Only users with the Cluster Administrator role can set this.

email.description: Email address to request password reset link for

position.description: array of per-finger [x,y] touch positions, up to 10 depending on model

start.description: array of per-finger [x,y] start touch positions, up to 10 depending on model

end.description: array of per-finger [x,y] end touch positions, up to 10 depending on model

expiresIn.description: Number of seconds token remains valid

features.description: The enabled UI features for this session

clientId.description: Optional string value supplied by client for tracking purposes

limit.description: The maximum number of items that could be returned for this result.

results.description: Array of network connections associated with this domain.

updatedBy.description: UUIDv4 of the user who last updated this record.
